parsed_review.summary: CP00-572 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack covering RP18.P06.M05.S29–RP18.P06.M06.S08 (the M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail, 2 rows, and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head, 8 rows; implementation 8 / security_audit 2). The pack baseline is internally consistent across manifest.json, registry CP572 binding (registry.js:358/1904), the contract current_pack and p06_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor (contract:16/22602), the closeout-pack-plan pack (closeout-pack-plan.json:406), and the model tests. The contract projection is deep-equality-validated against the generated descriptor by validate-rp18-ai-legal-workflows-core-contract.mjs (lines 1182-1187), so any drift would fail rp18:ai-legal-workflows-core:validate — recorded exit 0 in command-evidence.json along with all other pre-review gates (node --check/--test, rp00/rp05-rp18 validators, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/goal/fullplan validate, npm test/validate/build, git diff --check). The descriptor-only / no-real-data / no-write boundary is comprehensive: freezeCp572Result (service.js:2669) and the CP572 no-write attestation stamp every output with no-runtime, no-audit-write, no-permission-evaluation, and no-leak flags; every fixture row is a frozen descriptor with descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, and the inherited ROW_EXTRAS keys do not intersect CP572 row keys so no guard-override path exists. Authority boundary holds throughout — claude_is_final_approval:false, human approval required for runtime opening, no enterprise-trust claim from local validation (enforced in descriptor and checked at validators.js:3956-3963) — and Hermes is used only as a deterministic evidence-packet/receipt shape (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). The pack correctly remains production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review; the downstream review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are appropriately absent because they are produced after this read-only review in the hardening sequence. Auth preflight passed (readiness-report.json) and no prior invalid review attempts exist. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two P3 traceability observations are noted and require no action. This review is one gating step and is not final approval — pack and goal closeout remain gated on the remaining hardening steps and human approval.

findings.title: index.js listed as implementation_ref but unmodified — surfaces CP572 exports via wildcard re-export
findings.details: Every included unit lists packages/ai-legal-workflows/src/index.js in implementation_refs, but index.js is not in the changed-file set for this pack. Verified this is correct-by-design: index.js consists solely of `export * from "./registry.js"`, `export * from "./service.js"`, and `export * from "./validators.js"`, so the new CP572 functions (createAiLegalWorkflowsCoreCp572P06PermissionFixtureSliceDescriptor/CaseSet, validateAiLegalWorkflowsCoreCp572Coverage, etc.) surface automatically without editing index.js. The required_public_exports and index_export_check:true claims are satisfied. Not a defect; noted only because it is the one implementation_ref that does not appear in changed-file-scope.json and a future auditor may flag the absence.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Optionally document that index.js participates via wildcard re-export so the unmodified-but-referenced state is self-explanatory.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged non-blocking; verified correct via wildcard re-export. No change requested.

findings.title: production_ready_flag token name could be misread as a readiness assertion
findings.details: The manifest field production_ready_flag is set to the token ai_legal_workflows_core_p06_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified. The field name reads like a production-readiness assertion, but the actual boolean production_ready is false, runtime_ready is false, and implementation_layer is descriptor. The token denotes descriptor verification only and is the long-standing convention across CP00-551..CP00-572; the Hermes packet exposes production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid) which remains gated behind this review, adjudication, construction inspection, and human approval. Verified there is no over-claim of production readiness or enterprise trust introduced by this pack.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this pack. If desired program-wide, consider renaming the token field (e.g., descriptor_verification_flag) to reduce ambiguity, but this is cross-pack convention and out of scope for CP00-572.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged non-blocking; production_ready=false and no enterprise-trust claim. No change requested.
